text: The patient is positioned in a modified lithotomy position under spinal anesthesia. The size and extent of the rectocele are assessed before repair, and adrenalized saline is injected to decrease bleeding and facilitate dissection.
The surgeon begins the incision of the vaginal mucosa at the apex of the rectocele, extending distally towards the posterior fornix.
The surgeon carefully separates the vaginal mucosa from the underlying rectovaginal septum, revealing the distinct tissue layers during the dissection.
The surgeon marks the excess vaginal mucosa and excises it, removing the tissue from the surgical field.
The surgeon performs plication of the rectovaginal septum using two interrupted 2-0 absorbable sutures placed proximally, forming the first layer of the repair.
Two sutures are placed in the middle of the rectovaginal septum to secure the plication.
Two interrupted 2-0 absorbable sutures are placed distally near the posterior fourchette to secure the repair.
The surgical field shows the completed repair with sutures in place, demonstrating the closure of the mucosal incision and the plication of the rectovaginal septum.
